text.title: WebSocket Design Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: WebSocket URL contains ?token=eyJ... appearing in access logs.
antipattern.root-cause: Browsers cannot send custom headers on WebSocket upgrade; author chose the easy path.
antipattern.fix: Use a one-shot ticket from POST /ws-ticket exchanged for a session cookie or short-lived URL param that is logged-scrubbed.

antipattern.symptom: One misbehaving client floods the event loop and starves all other clients on the process.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated WebSocket like UDP — no per-connection ceiling.
antipattern.fix: Apply per-connection token bucket (e.g. 30 msgs/sec) and per-user connection cap (3–5).

antipattern.symptom: Deploy completes; every client reconnects at the same second and the auth endpoint falls over.
antipattern.root-cause: Linear or fixed-delay reconnect with no randomisation.
antipattern.fix: Replace with full-jitter exponential backoff capped at 30s.

antipattern.symptom: ConnectionManager state stored at module scope; horizontal scaling silently breaks fan-out.
antipattern.fix: Keep ConnectionManager per-process; push fan-out to Redis Pub/Sub.
